title: Week (September 1-5) in Review: Financial Markets
author: tickeron_community
selftext:
# Key Points

* **Market Volatility**: September began with significant selling pressure as bond yields soared globally, sending the [Dow Jones](https://tickeron.com/ticker/DIA/) down 250 points amid tariff uncertainties and fiscal concerns.
* **Gold Rally**: Gold reached a record high above $3,550 per ounce, driven by expectations of Federal Reserve rate cuts, a weakening dollar, and continued safe-haven demand.
* **Sterling Crisis**: The British pound crashed 1.3% as UK 30-year gilt yields surged to levels not seen since 1998, triggering fears about the UK's fiscal position.
* **Tech Sector Mixed**: [Apple](https://tickeron.com/ticker/AAPL/) surged nearly 4% on AI search tool news, while [Salesforce](https://tickeron.com/ticker/CRM/) tumbled 7% despite solid earnings due to weak guidance.
* **Jobs Disappointment**: August employment data showed only 22,000 new jobs added, well below the 75,000 expected, pushing unemployment to 4.3% and strengthening Fed rate cut expectations.
